message:
Running command: 
# The drainer isn't reading fast enough with the dd approach since dd blocks until `count` is reached.
# Use Python for a proper non-blocking reader

LIBPID=1100961

# Kill old drainers
kill 1101734 1102413 2>/dev/null || true

# Python pipe drainer - reads everything available continuously
nohup python3 -c "
import os, sys, time
PIPE = '/proc/$LIBPID/fd/11'
LOG = '/tmp/drt2_pipe.log'
with open(LOG, 'ab') as out:
    out.write(b'Python drainer started\n')
    out.flush()
    fd = os.open(PIPE, os.O_RDONLY | os.O_NONBLOCK)
    try:
        while True:
            try:
                data = os.read(fd, 65536)
                if data:
                    out.write(data)
                    out.flush()
                else:
                    time.sleep(0.01)
            except BlockingIOError:
                time.sleep(0.05)
            except OSError:
                break
    finally:
        os.close(fd)
        out.write(b'Python drainer exited\n')
        out.flush()
" > /dev/null 2>&1 &
echo "Python drainer: $!"

sleep 10
echo "=== $(date) ==="
wc -c /tmp/drt2_pipe.log
tail -20 /tmp/drt2_pipe.log
echo "---"
ps aux | grep openroad | grep -v grep | awk '{print "CPU:"$3"%","TIME:"$10}'

function.name: execute_bash
